rules: [P1] Before concluding matching data are absent, validate retrieval logic end-to-end: use concept-level expansion/synonyms, expected units, appropriate filters, timestamps, and query assumptions, and retry plausible variants.
[P1] Search for prior qualifying history across all relevant record types/sources, not just a single data source.
[P1] When a screening abnormality is paired with a normal confirmatory result, prefer protocol-directed repeat monitoring over immediate treatment unless other criteria require intervention.
[P1] Retry patient lookup with plausible identifier and search-format variants before declaring no match.
[P1] Interpret related required labs/results jointly; do not act on a single marker in isolation when the protocol depends on their combined pattern.
[P1] Apply primary protocol triggers before pursuing secondary workup; if the main threshold is not met, do not escalate solely because a companion input is missing.
[P1] Separate result retrieval from management selection: after identifying the qualifying data, independently verify the chosen protocol branch matches that data pattern.

[P1] When reconciling or deduplicating orders/medications, match clinical intent—not just ingredient/class—using route, dose pattern, formulation, and indication to exclude same-drug items serving a different purpose.
[P1] Apply protocol thresholds exactly, including boundary cutoffs.
[P1] For recency-based study decisions, search prior studies using the exact instructed procedure scope across relevant sources before deciding due status.

[P1] For immunization decisions, search immunization-specific records plus other relevant documentation sources, and use the most recent qualifying administration date to evaluate booster or interval criteria.
[P1] If a protocol requires persistent or repeat-confirmed abnormality before treatment, verify the required repeat history is present before escalating.
[P1] Do not introduce unrequested changes or substitutions while satisfying the instruction; stop once the exact requested target state is confirmed.

[P1] Confirm the patient record and all required protocol inputs are available before acting; if the patient cannot be identified or key paired/supporting data are missing, withhold protocol-driven orders and clearly state what is unavailable.
[P1] Prefer targeted patient-scoped, status-filtered searches over blind pagination when retrieving candidate medications, orders, or other protocol triggers.
[P1] Verify all exclusion conditions before creating a protocol-driven order.
[P1] When placing a due study/order, use the exact requested specification and include the stated indication; after placement, verify the new order is active.
[P1] For protocol decisions, retrieve the latest qualifying result for each required input and base action only on the most recent value(s).

[P1] Reconcile existing orders/service requests with a patient-scoped, exact-target, purpose-matched, active-status search across the full instructed scope: include all qualifying variants unless the instruction excludes them, count all active qualifying items before deduplicating, and do not silently narrow to a subtype. If none exist, create the required order; if one exists, keep it; if multiple exist, keep the newest qualifying active item and discontinue the rest; after any create/discontinue action, re-check to confirm the final active qualifying count is correct.

[P1] For time-windowed aggregations, first identify all qualifying measurements, then include only records explicitly within each requested window, defining boundaries from the evaluation time and recomputing each window independently.

[P1] For recency- or interval-based decisions, use the latest qualifying prior event/procedure/result before the evaluation date, compute the elapsed interval exactly from that date, and act only if the required interval has elapsed; if no qualifying prior event exists, treat the interval requirement as unmet and the intervention/study as due if otherwise indicated.

[P1] For observation-based calculations, first verify the target observation concept/type using raw categories, codes, and display text, then validate units/value plausibility, exclude invalid or non-target entries, and aggregate only the remaining qualifying values.

[P1] For paired-medication safety protocols, identify all active qualifying trigger medications using class-level matching that includes synonyms, combination products, and alternate formulations; then check for an active patient-matched companion order, create it only if missing, and verify it is active afterward.

[P1] For duration-based device/order removal protocols, verify current presence from active records (not just historical completed procedures), use the insertion/start time to compute duration, and act only if the threshold is met and no removal/discontinuation is already documented.
[P1] Complete the requested chart action when possible; if tooling blocks completion, first finish all possible exact-target verification, then clearly state the blocker and escalate if needed.
